root_text.an3.33:1.1: Atha kho āyasmā sāriputto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.an3.33:1.3: vitthārenapi kho ahaṁ, sāriputta, dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ;
root_text.an3.33:1.4: saṅkhittavitthārenapi kho ahaṁ, sāriputta, dhammaṁ deseyyaṁ;
root_text.an3.33:1.7: yaṁ bhagavā saṅkhittenapi dhammaṁ deseyya, vitthārenapi dhammaṁ deseyya, saṅkhittavitthārenapi dhammaṁ deseyya.
root_text.an3.33:2.2: ‘imasmiñca saviññāṇake kāye ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na bhavissanti, bahiddhā ca sabbanimittesu ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na bhavissanti, yañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ upasampajja viharato ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na honti tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ upasampajja viharissāmā’ti.
root_text.an3.33:3.1: Yato ca kho, sāriputta, bhikkhuno imasmiñca saviññāṇake kāye ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na honti, bahiddhā ca sabbanimittesu ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na honti, yañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ upasampajja viharato ahaṅkāramamaṅkāramānānusayā na honti tañca cetovimuttiṁ paññāvimuttiṁ upasampajja viharati;
root_text.an3.33:3.3: ‘bhikkhu acchecchi taṇhaṁ, vivattayi saṁyojanaṁ, sammā mānābhisamayā antamakāsi dukkhassa’.
root_text.an3.33:3.4: Idañca pana metaṁ, sāriputta, sandhāya bhāsitaṁ pārāyane udayapañhe:

translation_text.an3.33:1.1: Then Venerable Sāriputta went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.an3.33:1.4: maybe both in brief and in detail.
translation_text.an3.33:1.5: But it’s hard to find anyone who understands.”
translation_text.an3.33:1.6: “Now is the time, Blessed One! Now is the time, Holy One!
translation_text.an3.33:1.7: Let the Buddha teach Dhamma in brief, in detail, and both in brief and in detail.
translation_text.an3.33:1.8: There will be those who understand the teaching!”
translation_text.an3.33:2.1: “So, Sāriputta, you should train like this:
translation_text.an3.33:2.2: ‘There’ll be no I-making, mine-making, or underlying tendency to conceit for this conscious body; and no I-making, mine-making, or underlying tendency to conceit externally for all signs; and we’ll live having achieved the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where I-making, mine-making, and the underlying tendency to conceit are no more.’
translation_text.an3.33:3.1: When a mendicant has no I-making, mine-making, or underlying tendency to conceit for this conscious body; and no I-making, mine-making, or underlying tendency to conceit externally for all signs; and they live having attained the freedom of heart and freedom by wisdom where I-making, mine-making, and the underlying tendency to conceit are no more—
translation_text.an3.33:3.3: a mendicant who has cut off craving, cast off the fetters, and by rightly comprehending conceit has made an end of suffering.
translation_text.an3.33:3.4: And Sāriputta, this is what I was referring to in ‘The Way to the Far Shore’, in ‘The Questions of Udaya’ when I said:
translation_text.an3.33:5.3: this, I declare, is liberation by enlightenment,

variant_text.an3.33:3.3: acchecchi → acchejji (sya-all, km, mr) | vivattayi → vāvattayi (bj, pts1ed); vivaṭṭayi (sya-all)
